content:
First we index the elements of M by
  M₁=1, M₂=2, M₃=4, M₄=5,… in general
  M_{2t−1}=3t−2, M_{2t}=3t−1.
  
We look at 2n consecutive terms M_{j₀},…,M_{j₀+2n−1}.  One checks by pairing that their sum S is either

Case A (j₀ odd, say j₀=2k−1):
  S = 3n·(2k + n − 2) ,

Case B (j₀ even, say j₀=2k):
  S = 3n·(2k + n − 1) .

We want S=300 ⇒ 3n·(…)=300 ⇒ n·(…)=100.  Testing divisors n of 100 and forcing k∈ℕ gives exactly

Case A ⇒ n=2 or n=10,
Case B ⇒ n=1,4,5.

Hence the only possible values of n are

   n∈{1,2,4,5,10}.  
  
In particular there are five choices of n for which one can find 2n consecutive elements of M summing to 300.  

Answer:  \boxed{1,2,4,5,10}.
